x4freetext: Four different irradiation sites were used:
--- a thermal column (TC - nominal thermal and
--- In-core irradiation site, a Cd-lined site in the
--- In-core irradiation site, a second in the outer
The gamma rays were observed with high-purity
Ge detectors (nominal volume of 169 cm3, efficiency of
35% compared with NaI at 1332 keV, resolution of 1.68
keV at 1332 keV). Source-to-detector distances for the
stronger sources were initially 20 cm, moving to 15
cm as the source decayed. For the weaker sources the
counting started at 15 cm and later moved to 10 cm.
Se samples in the form of metal powder.
measurements lasted for 1 h in the GRICIT (GR) and
CLICIT (CL) sites and 2 h in the thermal column (TC),
and counting began about 2-3 h after the
(R) were of duration 1-10 min, and counting began
within 2-5 min for the short irradiations, in order
to see the short-lived activities, and within 10-30
